title: Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com at $20 billion valuation
dek: Citadel Securities deployed $400 million into Crypto.com in the Singapore-based exchange's first institutional funding round since 2016, valuing the firm at $20 billion. Crypto.com said it will use the capital to expand into tokenized securities, derivatives, prediction markets, and real-world assets.
author: Crypto Cronkite
key_fact: Citadel Securities, a tier-one Wall Street market maker, invested $400 million in Crypto.com at a $20 billion valuation, marking the exchange's first institutional capital infusion since its 2016 founding.
bottom_line: Watch whether other tier-one market makers follow Citadel into exchange equity and whether Crypto.com's stated expansion into tokenized securities and derivatives actually executes. The $20 billion valuation assumes success in asset classes still nascent and facing regulatory headwinds; if tokenized equities growth stalls or derivatives offerings face licensing delays, the valuation thesis weakens.
body: Citadel Securities, one of Wall Street's largest market makers, deployed $400 million into Crypto.com in the Singapore-based exchange's first institutional funding round since its 2016 founding. The deal values Crypto.com at $20 billion, according to CoinDesk's reporting.

The investment marks a notable entry point for tier-one institutional capital into crypto infrastructure. Citadel Securities manages roughly $290 billion in assets and executes a significant share of U.S. equity and options volume; its decision to commit $400 million to a crypto exchange signals confidence in the asset class as financial plumbing rather than speculative bet.

Crypto.com said it will deploy the capital to expand into tokenized securities, derivatives, prediction markets, and real-world assets (RWAs), according to CoinDesk's reporting. The exchange is developing offerings in these categories, the firm announced, though no timeline was disclosed. Crypto.com co-founder and CEO Kris Marszalek stated: "The size of the opportunity in front of us is staggering, as crypto increasingly becomes the rails for finance."

Wall Street firms have increasingly expanded into digital asset trading, tokenization, and custody since spot bitcoin ETFs were introduced in January 2024, per EY research cited by CoinDesk. The ETFs created a regulated on-ramp for institutional capital, lowering barriers for traditional finance firms to build crypto operations. Citadel Securities' entry into exchange equity signals that tier-one market makers now see crypto infrastructure as core business, not peripheral.

The deal structure, governance terms, board representation, and regulatory approvals required remain undisclosed. CoinDesk's reporting also did not specify whether this represents preferred or common equity, nor did it detail Citadel's expected governance role at the exchange. Crypto.com's previous internal valuations and whether this represents an increase or decrease from prior fundraising rounds were not reported.
